reference: GATE CS — Computer Networks / Transport & Data Link

formulas.name: Stop-and-Wait Efficiency
formulas.trap: Throughput = efficiency x bandwidth. Ignoring ACK time assumes ACK is tiny.

formulas.name: Sliding-Window Efficiency
formulas.plain: Sliding-window efficiency = min(1, W/(1+2a)); full link when W >= 1+2a
formulas.whenToUse: Throughput of GBN/SR with window size W.
formulas.trap: Window needed for 100% utilization is W = 1 + 2a (round up).

formulas.name: GBN vs Selective-Repeat Window
formulas.plain: With m-bit sequence numbers: Go-Back-N window <= 2^m - 1, Selective Repeat window <= 2^(m-1)
formulas.whenToUse: Choosing max window for a given sequence-number field.
formulas.trap: Exceeding these limits causes the receiver to mistake retransmissions for new frames.

formulas.name: TCP AIMD
formulas.plain: TCP congestion avoidance: additive increase (+1 MSS/RTT), multiplicative decrease (halve on loss)
formulas.trap: Slow start grows cwnd exponentially (doubles per RTT) until ssthresh, then AIMD linear.

formulas.name: Token Bucket Burst Time
formulas.plain: Max burst duration S = C/(M - rho), where C=bucket capacity, M=max output rate, rho=token rate
formulas.trap: Max bytes sent in burst of length S = C + rho*S; valid only while M > rho.
